Aasimar.name: Aasimar
traits: Celestial Resistance: Resistance to Necrotic and Radiant damage.[cite: 10]
Healing Hands: As an action, restore HP equal to d4s equal to your Proficiency Bonus.[cite: 10]
Celestial Revelation: At 3rd level, choose Heavenly Wings, Inner Radiance, or Necrotic Shroud.[cite: 10]
Aasimar.proctorHint: Ideal for Support/Blasters. Use Heavenly Wings at 3rd level to gain the high ground.[cite: 10]

Dragonborn.name: Dragonborn
traits: Breath Weapon: Replace one attack with an exhalation of draconic energy.[cite: 10]
Draconic Flight: At 5th level, sprout wings as a bonus action for 1 minute.[cite: 10]
Dragonborn.proctorHint: Optimized for Blasters. Replacing an attack with a Breath Weapon allows you to maintain high action economy.[cite: 10]

Dwarf.name: Dwarf
traits: Dwarven Resilience: Advantage on saves against poison; resistance to poison damage.[cite: 10]
Stonecunning: Double proficiency bonus on History checks related to stonework.[cite: 10]
Subraces: Hill Dwarf (+1 max HP/level) or Mountain Dwarf (Light/Medium Armor prof).[cite: 10]
Dwarf.proctorHint: A premier choice for Frontline Controllers. Heavy armor compatibility provides stability.[cite: 10]

Elf.name: Elf
traits: Fey Ancestry: Advantage on saves against being Charmed; magic cannot put you to sleep.[cite: 10]
Trance: Meditate for 4 hours to gain the benefits of a Long Rest.[cite: 10]
Lineages: High Elf (Cantrip swap), Wood Elf (35 ft. Speed), or Drow (120 ft. Darkvision).[cite: 10]
Elf.proctorHint: The ultimate versatile Controller. High Elves can swap their cantrip daily to adapt.[cite: 10]

Gnome.name: Gnome
traits: Gnome Cunning: Advantage on all INT, WIS, and CHA saves against magic.[cite: 10]
Lineages: Forest Gnome (Minor Illusion) or Rock Gnome (Tinker devices).[cite: 10]
Gnome.proctorHint: Tailored for Blasters. Gnome Cunning provides unmatched resilience against spellcasters.[cite: 10]

Goliath.name: Goliath
traits: Giant Ancestry: Choose a heritage (Cloud, Fire, Frost, Hill, Stone, Storm) for a specific combat benefit.[cite: 10]
Large Form: At 5th level, become Large as a bonus action for 1 minute.[cite: 10]
Goliath.proctorHint: Built for Melee Controllers. Hill Giant Ancestry allows you to knock enemies prone on a hit.[cite: 10]

Halfling.name: Halfling
traits: Lucky: Reroll a natural 1 on any d20 roll for an attack, check, or save.[cite: 10]
Brave: Advantage on saves against being Frightened.[cite: 10]
Halfling Nimbleness: Move through the space of any creature larger than you.[cite: 10]
Naturally Stealthy: Attempt to hide when obscured by a creature at least one size larger.[cite: 10]
Halfling.proctorHint: Perfect for Support. The Lucky trait significantly reduces the risk of critical failures.[cite: 10]

Human.name: Human
traits: Base Human: All six Ability Scores increase by 1.[cite: 10]
Variant Human: Increase two Ability Scores by 1. Gain one Skill proficiency and one Feat.[cite: 10]
Human.proctorHint: The 'Jack-of-all-Trades.' Selecting a Feat at Level 1 allows you to specialize in a role.[cite: 10]

Orc.name: Orc
traits: Adrenaline Rush: Bonus action to Dash and gain Temporary HP.[cite: 10]
Powerful Build: Count as one size larger for carrying capacity and pushing/dragging.[cite: 10]
Relentless Endurance: When reduced to 0 HP, drop to 1 HP instead (Once per Long Rest).[cite: 10]
Orc.proctorHint: A top-tier choice for Support/Healers. Adrenaline Rush allows you to quickly reach a downed ally.[cite: 10]

Owlin.name: Owlin
Owlin.description: Avian humanoids with diverse physiological traits suited for both the academic rigors and tactical demands of Strixhaven.[cite: 8]
traits: Flight: You have a flying speed equal to your current walking speed.[cite: 8]
Silent Feathers: You have proficiency in the Stealth skill.[cite: 8]
Owlin.proctorHint: Excellent for scouting and vertical mobility across the Strixhaven campus.

Tiefling.name: Tiefling
traits: Hellish Resistance: Resistance to Fire damage.[cite: 10]
Otherworldly Gaze: Gain Thaumaturgy, Hellish Rebuke, and Darkness.[cite: 10]
Tiefling.proctorHint: Natural fit for Blasters. Casting Darkness allows for consistent battlefield control.[cite: 10]
